message:
feat(verifier_sdk): replace !sig.is_empty() with real ed25519 signature verification for migration artifacts

The migration artifact verifier accepted any non-empty string as a valid
signature, contradicting the cryptographic trust model. Replace the
placeholder with real Ed25519 signature verification:

Key changes:
- New compute_capsule_integrity_hash() with domain-separated
  length-prefixed preimage for capsule binding
- New verify_ed25519_signature_hex() using ed25519_dalek for real
  signature verification with hex-encoded keys and signatures
- New canonical_migration_artifact_payload() that strips signature fields
  before canonicalization to avoid circular signing
- is_sha256_hex() validates content_hash shape including "sha256:" prefix
- verify_migration_artifact() now checks signature_algorithm == "ed25519"
  and calls verify_ed25519_signature_hex with the signer_public_key
- Assertion renamed from "signature_present" to "signature_valid"
- content_hash validation uses is_sha256_hex() instead of length == 64
- Binding hash computed over canonical payload (not full artifact with sig)

Test updates:
- test_signing_key() and sign_migration_artifact() helpers for test fixtures
- test_verify_migration_artifact_pass: now signs artifacts before verification
- test_verify_migration_artifact_deterministic: uses real signatures
- New test_verify_migration_artifact_tampered_signature_fails: verifies
  that changing content_hash after signing causes Verdict::Fail

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
